title: Real-Gas Equation of State: Extrapolate a Cylinder EOS into the High-Pressure Regime
strategies: constant/low-order baseline: two-term virial fit P = k*T/V + c/V**2 (captures dilute-gas trend, no excluded-volume pole -> mis-extrapolates at high pressure)
van-der-Waals recovery: P = k*T/(V-b) - a/V**2, co-volume b by 1-D search + linear least squares; the pressure pole at V=b is the dominant high-pressure physics -> beats the virial fit
Redlich-Kwong recovery: P = k*T/(V-b) - a/(sqrt(T)*V*(V+b)); matches the hidden law's temperature-dependent attraction and volume shape -> best extrapolation, still noise-limited (headroom remains)
invalid: emit a non-expression / disallowed token / non-finite output
